query:
So... I want your assessment of the most efficient & safe way of cleaning up these PR's... don't start anything.. just assess and come up with a plan (bugfix protocol, independently, feature scope): Batch cleanup PR — small ready-to-fix bugs:                                                  
                                                                  
  These are all tiny, no design needed, could ship as one PR:
                                                                                               
  - #337 merge_configs doesn't re-validate InferenceConfig                                     
  - #345 saturating_add for histogram counter                                                  
  - #346 sanitize_session_id in CompactPayload                                                 
  - #378 escape markdown in cycle review formatter                
  - #379 pipe-escape category/feature_cycle in Knowledge Reuse table                           
  - #380 misleading comment on obs.ts cast

entries.title: ADR-005 crt-025: outcome Category Retirement — Ingest Block Only, No Data Deletion
ADR-005 col-020b: Time-Boxed #193 Investigation with Store-Layer Scope Boundary
Clean first-pass delivery from precise scope decomposition and file-aligned parallelism
Multi-pass cleanup: all passes on the same reference table must apply identical status filters
Verify reported bug scope is still live before diagnosing: gates can be silently removed and components can be removed from pipelines
